title: author-agent-spec round-trip validation does not catch resolvePromptField misclassification — false positive at author-time
description: author-agent-spec validates its written spec by round-tripping through parseAgentYaml at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/author-agent-spec-tool.ts line 76 (the investigation report cites line 93 in its read; line numbers vary by file version). When a prompt is misclassified by resolvePromptField (per FGAP-153/FB-001), parseAgentYaml succeeds — the value is stored in systemPromptTemplate / taskPromptTemplate (a string field), which is structurally valid AgentSpec content. Validation reports success. The failure surfaces only later at compileAgent's renderTemplateFile call as ENOENT. The validation gate is shape-only — no semantic check that template-path values resolve to existing files or to `block:` sentinels.

evidence.reference: FB-003 'author-agent-spec validation is a false positive for misclassified prompts'; actual_behavior: spec with 'Read docs/03-solar-engine.md' as inline task prompt passes author-agent-spec validation; call-agent fails with ENOENT trying to open the prompt as a file

Investigation report FB-003 section confirms validation call site verbatim; verdict 'Code + Validation (dual-layer)' — code fix per FB-001 eliminates the misclassification; validation layer adds semantic check that template paths exist or are `block:` sentinels
parseAgentYaml entry point invoking resolvePromptField on spec.prompt.system and spec.prompt.task — no semantic validation after field resolution

impact: Authors receive author-agent-spec success messages for specs that will hard-fail at first dispatch. The corrupted state persists in the substrate until call-agent surfaces ENOENT. The validation gate's promise (round-trip succeeds = spec is dispatchable) is broken by the heuristic-classification chain in FGAP-153/154. Closing FGAP-153 (the heuristic) eliminates the misclassification entirely and makes this gap moot for the inline-prompt case; this gap stands separately as the structural absence of semantic validation in author-agent-spec for any template-path field.
proposed_resolution: Code at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/agent-spec.ts (resolvePromptField narrowing per FGAP-153 closure) eliminates the heuristic-driven misclassification. Defense-in-depth at packages/pi-agent-dispatch/src/author-agent-spec-tool.ts: after parseAgentYaml round-trip, verify that any template-path field (systemPromptTemplate, taskPromptTemplate) either resolves to an existing file or matches a `block:` sentinel. Throw at author-time when neither condition holds. The semantic-check layer also catches user-authored template paths that point to genuinely-missing files.
